title: lithocarpus reflexus
intro: Lithocarpus reflexus, also known as the Quaking Cork, is a species of flowering plant in the beech family that is native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the management of respiratory issues. Lithocarpus reflexus is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal extracts or infusions, which can be taken as teas, capsules, or applied topically in the form of ointments or creams.
